reviews.review_description:
I bought this a LONG time ago. I hadn't had my quest very long, but long enough to look for the not so popular games. They used to have a category i can't remember what is was called. But it had all these cheaper games, that weren't the big titles. A few that I bought, including this one have been some of my all time favorites. The puzzles were fun. Not too hard, but challenging enough to enjoy. It was just a really cool and nice looking game for the price. I got an alert that there is going to be new content after all this time. I'm very excited for that. Can't wait!!
I've seen alot of reviews of people saying it was short. It wasn't long, but it wasn't short. Definitely the right amount of content for the price.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We're really excited to launch the new version in a few weeks.

reviews.review_description:
I played this game a year ago. I found enjoyed it quite a bit. It was on the shorter side, but the graphics were good and the puzzles were just the right amount of challenging. 
  Then I see in my news feed that it’s getting more rooms this June!! That would fix my only complaint with this game. I just wanted more of it. I’ll be hopping back in the moment the additional content releases 😁

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We're excited to hear what you think of the new puzzles and content!

reviews.review_description:
Could have been fun but I don’t play in one sitting. Every time I leave the game I come back to a non working puzzle where I left off ( the one I did last ). I tried it multiple times , at different locations, the same thing happens every single time and I have to start the game all over again. It’s clearly just not working.

Upd: so after endless tries I had no options but to play it in one sitting and I can’t describe my disappointment- the game is extremely SHORT!!  The game FOUR rooms! That’s it. And they are quite easy. With 2 rooms I already did a million times and two rooms that were new to me it took me less than an hour to finish… the whole game would have been 2 hours. 

This whole game is a disappointment. 

Also it doesn’t adjust the night properly in a sitting mode.

I would love to reply to your comments but there’s no any contact info.

developer_response.body:
We're sorry to hear this was your experience. We've never experienced this particular bug before so it is hard to track. Give us a clue to what room and puzzle it is so we can pinpoint the issue. 

Separately, we are releasing an update at new further charge in June with more rooms and puzzles.

reviews.review_description: If you like the atmosphere of cinemas, you will definitely enjoy it. It is one of the shortest games, but it is fun, with many tasks. Maybe a few more rooms and tasks (e.g. ticket office, basement, attic, bathroom) would have been good, but the length of the game was still appropriate for the story.

developer_response.body: Thank you!

reviews.review_description: Great puzzles and atmosphere! This is exactly the kind of game I’ve been looking for on the Quest. Thanks!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Look for some new updates SOON! :-)

reviews.review_title: Great game but ending is weird
reviews.review_description:
I liked the game. But the game ended very weird or something was wrong.
I got the gun out of the safe,  while checking how to get back to the lobby the game ended.
If this was correct then the game ends disappointingly . Like movies or books. Put all energy in good plot but no thoughts of a good ending. It did not feel like a cliff hanger hinting a 2nd part might come. 
2 stars based on the ending.

developer_response.body:
If you have some more time, we're launching an update in the coming weeks that addresses this particular note!

Check out some of our new puzzles and materials when the new ending launches soon.

reviews.review_description: My popcorn scale won’t budge so I can’t complete the COMBO task. As another person had the same problem and resolved it by starting a new game. I’m on my third attempt with a NEW game, so fingers crossed this works and I can move on.

developer_response.body: I'm sorry to hear this. We've seen this happen once in a while but its not the typical experience for a player. Were you able to move past this?

reviews.review_description:
It's a great game, it's intriguing with an interesting storyline, the puzzles are a mixed bag; some are very, glaringly obvious in their solution, others slightly more obscure... It does make you think a bit... But not too much. Good as it is, it does have its problems: Frequently my hands (with disturbingly tiny thumbs) got stuck inside objects, such as tables and some units with which I had to interact. There are also some objects you could only pick up "at range" which ALWAYS resulted in the object flying across the room after inevitably getting stuck under a shelf or countertop.
It is VERY short, very disappointingly so, considering its price.

developer_response.body: Check out the updates we're about to launch to the game with extended gameplay. Thanks for your support!

reviews.review_title: Good game, but stuck in lobby
reviews.review_description: This is a real fun game but I’m struck on the lion near the snack bar. I can’t move the cables anymore and the lights are all green. Nothing happened. The side of the lion did not open.

developer_response.body: Please check out the update to our game in the coming weeks with some updates and new material.

reviews.review_description: This was a great VR puzzle game.  Is was a short game, but the graphics and puzzles were a lot fun.  Would definitely recommend.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the support!

Be sure to check out the update we're going to publish in the coming weeks!

reviews.review_description: Amazing just wish it was longer but that is true for every amazing game!

developer_response.body: You will soon get your wish!

reviews.review_description: This game I thought was a good game I thought I was just getting started to find out the end was very near this game it focused between very few rooms to explore ending was really quick as well as game don’t waste your money or time you will be disappointed

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. Check out the update we're posting to the game in the coming weeks!

reviews.review_description:
I love escape room games. Every time I'm curious about what puzzles the creators will put before us and here I was not disappointed. I try to avoid using hints but here I had to use them a few times, and the hint system is great.

The game has a great atmosphere, looks nice and is addictive. I recommend it to every escape room lover

Cannot reach items - or need to switch heights constantly, usually to no avail. Even with height change - the organ was impossible, either too short and cannot see or too tall and are reaching for the floor while sitting. The atmosphere is great, and the puzzles are great. Gripping an item is extremely problematic. Could not finish because of frustrating design issues.

developer_response.body: Sorry you've had this experience. We haven't heard this feedback before from several thousands of players. We'll definitely look into it!

reviews.review_title: Immersive but too short for the cost
reviews.review_description: I'm torn.  It's a really cool environment and except for one bonkers problem, I was able to work it out myself.  My low rating comes because I felt it had only just begun (literally four areas) before it was over.  There was so much that could have been revealed. I felt it was set up to be a long, involved process and the game was over in the space of a couple of hours.  When the demo gets you through almost half the game, you know it's too short.  So GREAT vibe, cool puzzles, reasonable hints when needed, just too danged abrupt on the ending. This is a max $10 experience.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback. Remember, creating a game is such an expensive and time consuming process. We always want to add material and we'd love to add more to the game and plan to do so in the near future. 

That said, if you love the game that we created so far, it would be great to get some feedback on new areas you'd like to see and also to rate us a little higher based on content and not necessarily on the time of the game itself.

reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed this escape room app. I needed a few hints and appreciated the ability to get one when I needed a little help. The puzzles were challenging and diverse. The story and puzzles were unique. I would love to play more!

The game is an awesome escape game experience set in an historic cinema which is named "The Atlas". The atmosphere is heavily influenced by the the golden age of cinema in the 1920's. You can interact with nearly every object in the game, therefore the game feels pretty interactive and has the look and feel of a real escape game where messing around and trying out stuff is always a good strategy. Written handouts are telling the underlying lore.

The german localization is the game is professionally made and pretty good. A clear recommendation for every escape room fan

I jus got started, made it to the lobby shoppe, but the popcorn scale stopped working. I exited, turned off the quest, and restarted it, still the popcorn scale won't work, and I can't get past the "making a combo" without it! What do I do?? How can I fix this? Or can I get a refund since it doesn't work?
I started over with a new game, since it was close to the start, and it worked this time. But if I go into the other saved game, the needle on the scale just won't move, it does nothing. Started over, just finished it, and next to the 7th guest, this us my second favorite vr game. Lots of fun, wish it lasted longer.

developer_response.body:
UPDATED Response:

Thanks for that heads up! And I appreciate you starting the game over again. We'll look into the save system and what is happening there.

reviews.review_description: Great escape room style game that has a sense of intrigue and theming. No jump scares or frustrating puzzles. Just a fun hour or two of solid  gameplay.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the support!

reviews.review_title: Minor issues but overall a good game
reviews.review_description: I would prefer specific locations to teleport to instead of ending up inside objects or way too close to walls etc.   The crouching option is kind of annoying when you all of a sudden drop to the floor.  The controller is difficult to use, grabbing items are hit and miss.  So far I've figured out a few things and am not sure how far into the game I've gotten but will enjoy continuing to figure out everything.  I don't find this game creepy at all.

Rarely does a game immerse me in a dark environment and I believe it's a big room ahead of me and not just two dark LCD screens. Between the music, atmosphere and story line I was fully invested in this game and wanted to learn and solve the mystery. 

The game does have some flaws, some puzzles can be pretty hard for people who don't play a lot of puzzlers. Your hands have physics with the in game objects which can lead to them getting stuck or not acting as you hope.

Overall the game has some awesome environments, great scaling of size, numerous puzzles that aren't overly hard or easy, and a tense environment that keeps you engaged in it's mystery. An escape room/puzzler that's worth checking out!

reviews.review_title: Great Puzzles With An Interesting Story
reviews.review_description:
You can see this game in my top 10 app lab list here: https://youtu.be/bwGl1PFWceA

Or just search 'VR Reviews' in YouTube.

The devs have made a great escape-room type puzzler here. I found the puzzles to be fairly challenging and the atmosphere and story drew me in. It might last you about 2 hours or so and is well worth the price of admission.

reviews.review_title: If you like The Room VR, you'll like it
reviews.review_description:
Deserves recognition to be pushed to the regular Quest store and here's why:
Short Spoiler-free review here - https://youtu.be/qmhWcg60oOw

developer_response.body: Thank you! That's a big compliment.

reviews.review_description:
The Atlas Mystery is a budget puzzler with fair bit of charm and a decent little story.  Minus only a few puzzles, the ones in here are unique which is something I always appreciate, and many are thematically linked to the theatre making them feel authentic for the setting.  If you love VR puzzlers, The Atlas Mystery won’t blow you away, but it’s not one to miss either and offers up a very satisfying VR puzzle solving experience.

VIDEO REVIEW - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GjBVr2X4NAk

WRITTEN REVIEW - https://www.thevrgrid.com/the-atlas-mystery/

I enjoyed the puzzles and felt satisfied when I completed one, there were a couple that had me stuck for a bit, but figured them out in the end. 
There are some glitches that were a bit annoying ( one where 1 of 2 interlocked pieces disappeared before I figured out what it was for, but luckily I remembered what it showed to solve it) and the interaction between the controls and puzzles was a little sloppy, hands tend to get stuck on things, but overall was a fun puzzler that I recommend to any puzzle fans.

I've been looking forward to playing this for a little while, but the framerate on the quest 1 is quite bad, to the point where I started feeling sick after a few minutes of gameplay. I'm hoping to continue, but not sure if I can :(

developer_response.body: We're sorry you had a bad experience with Quest 1. The game is really optimized for Quest 2 and since it is on AppLab, we can't specify that it's really only a Quest 2 game. We're sorry about that but that is a Meta setting. We'd be happy to help find a way for you to play it on Steam.

reviews.review_description: LOVE the game, but I am too frustrated to continue playing until the issues are fixed. There are so many glitches it's just too annoying at this time. I won't return it; I'll shelve it for now and wait for updates. To bad it wasn't player ready when I paid my money to play.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback, Ellynn. I'm sorry you had some many glitches in your playthrough. As developers we want everyone to have the best experience possible and that's why we've put out 4 patches to address some low hanging fruit. If you can, please send us your thoughts to our support email info@toprightcorner.com. We appreciate your time!

reviews.review_description: Havnt played a VR game in a while that has kept me wanting to play as much as this game. Didn't want to stop playing! Great game!

This is such a fun game to play 
The puzzles are the right type of difficulty , just enough to keep you guessing but not to hard to put you off . 
I highly recommend this game 

Martin76x

developer_response.body: Thanks, Martin!

reviews.review_description: Really enjoyed this game with just the right amount of challenge to keep me on edge, but not infuriate me. Atlas Mystery strikes a great balance of sharing clues and information to move the story and game forward without feeling like you are totally stuck, but it's also not too easy. Good variety of puzzle challenges and lots of physical elements to interact with. I played on Quest and Quest 2 and definitely recommend the latter. My only wish is that they make a multiplayer game next because I kept wanting to invite my family in to help with puzzles!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We pushed performance towards Quest 2 and Meta is phasing out Quest 1 right now. We are exploring multiplayer on titles and other TRC games to come. Really appreciate the feedback!

reviews.review_description: I really liked playing this game. The puzzles were really fun and creative, and it took me like 2hrs which is pretty decent. Over all, the story was good but it like only got to the player in therms of reading documents, and not by interacting or finding things that relate to the story. The graphics are okay for quest 2, but nothing special really. The sound design is really good, allthough its missing some variation at a few times. What i didnt like was missing interactions, with basically everything that didnt belong to a puzzle. If a thing isnt relevant to a puzzle, you mostly cant interact. By interact i mean, grabbing an object. There are just too many objects that are frozen and cant be moved, it just breaks the immersion sometimes. Also, you can cheat, very easily. You can look through walls, because the game fades into black way to slow. You can put your hand into safes/ objects you should only acces with a code, if you only apply enough pressure to it. In general, it feels a little unpolished because of missing menu click sounds or a cursor showing you where you click at (in menus). But over all,its worth the money if you are into the genre. Considering developing for vr is really difficult, the devs did a great job at making this game. I had lots of fun playing it :D

Loved this game, some tricky puzzles ,and some easier.Loved the graphics, background music was pleasant (not overwhelming like some games). I enjoyed the graphics, I had a few problems in sitting mode in the “hidden safe “ room re . found it very hard to read the combination dials.Possibly my headset or eyeballs! I did stop playing for a couple of days and when I started again I spent a happy hour searching for an object which I thought I must have dropped somewhere THEN remembered I had a rucksack !! All in all great fun ,wish it had gone on longer. Part 2 maybe? Highly recommended

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! Your support means a lot to us as independent developers. 

We hope to get onto Meta's main store and then expand the game further!

reviews.review_description:
Found this game because the company sent me a DM. 

It's a decent puzzle game. Some puzzles were way too easy for me, others were quite challenging so I was ready to look for solutions online but then managed to figure it out!

The physics is quite realistic which is good.

One thing that I disliked was that the rooms felt too big. It's a grand theatre yes, but still the rooms felt too big..

I really enjoyed the game, but it’s really glitchy when it comes to the controls. Firstly if you want to play seated then at a lot of the puzzles you either, can’t see them because they’re so far above your head that you’ve no choice but to stand up. But when you do you're then towering over the play space where the puzzle is. I was constantly going into the settings and changing from ‘sitting’ to ‘standing’ because of this. This I’m sure, is a terrible experience for disabled people who have no choice and perhaps cannot stand. The feature where you can click one of the buttons to lower yourself to ground level didn’t help either. I also found that when you’re trying to grab onto something, my hands would lock onto handles, popcorn scoop, literally anything I was trying to hold and just freeze. It was so frustrating, this is something I’ve seen in other reviews too. Another glitch I found also is that I would just float backwards while I was at a puzzle and would constantly have to propel myself forwards to keep in the play space. This would happen most by the organ and I was continually propelled backward off of the stage.  The game itself was fun, the graphics were good and definitely made you feel like you were in a lovely old theatre. The story was interesting to follow but I was slightly disappointed by the ending as I thought it was a little anticlimactic, but hey that’s just my opinion and overall I’m glad I played it. The Atlas Mystery has huge potential and once the glitches are sorted out, this game could be a real winner.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback Annie. Really appreciate that and we'll look to make some changes in V1.9

reviews.review_description: I love a puzzle and this game fulfilled that need until I got to the organ and it stopped working properly.  First, the flashlight disappeared and without the flashlight you cannot get a very important component to solve the next problem.  I’d hoped when I stopped the game it would appear the next time I opened the game…nope, it did not.  Then after I got the pick, which is also a very important component to completing the game, I had to stop the game shortly after retrieving it (due to low battery), so I sat it down where I could pick it back up and, again, nope…nowhere to be found.  So in order for me to complete the game, I still need the flashlight, which is gone, and the pick, which is also gone.  I guess I have to start from the beginning, which I am not going to do at this moment.  Maybe when I get down right desperate.

developer_response.body: I'm really sorry to hear that. We just launched a new update v1.9 that has many patches and visual updates. The development team is committed to updating and polishing this game further. We'd love for you to revisit the game and potentially change your view if you think it has improved!

reviews.review_description: I just started a series about this game on my YouTube channel, Bigman89, and so far the game is very intriguing.  I've been a fan of puzzle/mystery games ever since I started vr. If you're a fan of these types of games, then you should definitely  check this game out.

The Atlas Mystery is a classic escape room game in every sense of the word. Whereas other ‘escape room VR games’ do things in virtual reality that simply would not be possible in real life, The Atlas Mystery takes another approach: it pushes the players to do exactly things they would do in real life, but in a virtual setting. Funnily enough, this style of gameplay was oddly refreshing. I found myself pushed to gently twisting dials with a shaky hand, holding up film negatives to the light, unplugging and rewiring complex panels, and even using a handheld shovel to scoop freshly popped popcorn into a cup. Yes, really!

My full review can be found here
https://theescaperoomer.com/the-altas-mystery-vr-review/

reviews.review_title: This game is one of the best games I own
reviews.review_description: This game is for all kind of gamers, thrill seekers, Puzzle masters, and Ghost Hunters. This game has numerous and amazing puzzles and is similar Red Matter. Play this game you will never regret it.

I don’t like horror games very much and at first glance thought this game was such. Although it was a little creepy having to enter into a few dark places and not knowing what’s ahead, it was not bad at all. No jump scares and nothing frightening. 

Here’s a few reasons why I gave it 4 stars:

1) it was a little hard to determine exactly where to start at some points of the game. 

2) if and when you do get stuck you are wondering around for a while trying to pick up clues to what you need. A hint button is desperately needed. 

3) once you get to the puzzles they are very well thought out. Not to hard and not to easy! The perfect combination. 

4) the length of the game is very short. I was able to finish it with about a total of 4- 5hrs. If your REALLY good at puzzles you could probably complete it within 1-2 hrs. I did get lost a few a times but after throughly searching I was able to find my way. 

Would I recommend this game? Absolutely!!! but do keep in mind, if puzzles are not your strong suit then allow yourself some time to complete this game.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback!

reviews.review_description: The game sets you out into a long since shutdown theatre… alone with many fun puzzles.  Touches upon liminal spaces so it feels sorta creepy, which isn’t unwarranted based on the games story.  Gameplay and story both are rewarding and leave you wanting more! 5/5
